pre-tool-use-rules.name: xcodebuild_command_constraint
pre-tool-use-rules.message: Make sure to use the `xcodebuild` skill to compile with xcodebuild.

pre-tool-use-rules.name: swift_build_test_command_constraint
pre-tool-use-rules.message: Make sure to use the `swift-package` skill to compile with `swift build`/`swift test`.

pre-tool-use-rules.name: xcodebuild_with_project
pre-tool-use-rules.message: Unless the project doesn't have a workspace, make sure to use the `-workspace` flag instead of `-project` when invoking xcodebuild.

versions.changes: Sandbox wrappers renamed bin/xcodebuild-sandbox -> bin/xcodebuild and bin/swift-sandbox -> bin/swift to shadow /usr/bin, so nested xcodebuild/swift calls from Makefiles, fastlane, and other build scripts are sandboxed transparently. inject-session-id hook now prepends the plugin bin/ to PATH (Claude Code appends plugin bins, which left the wrappers behind /usr/bin), exports CLAUDE_SESSION_ID, and exports SANDBOX_DERIVED_DATA / SANDBOX_PACKAGES pointing at the per-session DerivedData and SPM paths for scripts that need to read build products or cached checkouts.
inject-session-id PreToolUse hook now only exports CLAUDE_SESSION_ID when a Bash command references xcodebuild-sandbox or swift-sandbox, instead of prefixing every Bash command

Static sandbox wrappers (bin/xcodebuild-sandbox, bin/swift-sandbox) replace dynamically generated scripts; sandbox keyed by Claude session_id (injected via PreToolUse hook as $CLAUDE_SESSION_ID) instead of $PPID; added SessionEnd hook for sandbox cleanup
Removed auto-update hook from SessionStart to prevent configuration issues
Add --executable flag to xcsift in swift-package and xcode-test skills for consistent executable path output
Restored auto-update hook; version tracking now uses CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA with migration from old .claude/ directory

Build isolation sandbox: CLI builds now use isolated DerivedData and SPM cache via wrapper scripts, preventing conflicts with Xcode. Always active — transparent when Xcode MCP handles builds, automatic when falling back to CLI.
Removed auto-update hook from SessionStart
Xcode MCP compatibility: auto-detects Xcode 26.3+ native MCP server and delegates overlapping tools (build, test, project inspection, docs). Adds auto-approve hook for MCP authorization dialog
Improved sparkle-integration: separate Debug/Release entitlements, @Observable pattern, Claude CLI release notes generation
Added sparkle-integration skill for integrating Sparkle 2.x auto-update framework into macOS apps
Fixed hook deduplication issue when multiple plugins installed - each plugin now has its own hooks.json
Improved skill matching to support PluginName:skill prefix format in addition to skills array
Added skills array to info.json; improved skill matching in pre-tool-use hook
Changed launch-app-device.py and stop-app-device.py to use --app instead of --bundle-id; bundle ID now auto-extracted via PlistBuddy
Added pre-tool-use rule to warn about using -project instead of -workspace; improved skill/bash matching in common infrastructure
Added guidance to use xcrun simctl list to find valid simulator IDs instead of guessing
Added guidance to use -workspace instead of -project when workspace exists (xcodebuild and xcode-test skills)
Improved regex pattern for swift build/test detection using word boundaries instead of trailing whitespace
Split pre-tool-use rule into separate rules for xcodebuild and swift build/test, directing to appropriate skills
Add --executable flag to xcsift commands for displaying built executable path
Added configurable pre-tool-use rules with allow/deny/deny_once decisions and regex matching
Added single-line command constraint to xcodebuild and xcode-test skills for consistency
Version tracker now silently initializes on first run instead of showing entire version history
Fixed hooks path to use plugin-local common directory (plugins cannot reference external files)
Centralized hooks to common/hooks.json and made scripts self-configuring from plugin.json
Renamed from XcodeBuildSkills to XcodeBuildTools. Added version tracking with changelog notifications and session hooks.
Initial release with 8 Xcode development tools
